workflow.name: Send personalized emails from Google Sheets via SMTP
workflow.description:
# What problem does this solve?

This workflow automates sending personalized emails using a contact list stored in Google Sheets and a professional SMTP email account.

It removes manual work such as identifying who should receive an email, filtering no-show or specific leads, avoiding duplicate sends, and updating records after emails are sent. The entire process runs automatically in a controlled and repeatable way.

This is useful when you want to send targeted emails without using a full email marketing platform.

## How this workflow operates

This workflow works with any type of email list stored in Google Sheets and sends emails based on conditions you define.

1. No-show workshop or webinar attendees
Mark contacts as No-Show in the sheet and the workflow will send personalized follow-up emails only to those contacts.

2. Cold email lists
Use the same workflow for cold outreach by adjusting the filter condition (for example, status = “Cold”).

3. Old or re-engagement lists
Re-engage inactive contacts by updating their status (for example, “Re-engage” or “Inactive”) and running the workflow.

4. Custom filtering logic
The filter conditions can be customized to match any column value (status, tag, campaign, or label), giving you full control over who receives emails.

In all cases, emails are sent one by one, duplicate sends are prevented, and Google Sheets is updated after each successful send.

## What does it do?

1. Fetches contact data from Google Sheets

2. Processes contacts one by one to ensure safe delivery

3. Filters contacts based on a no-show or custom status
 
4. Checks whether an email has already been sent

5. Sends personalized emails via SMTP
 
6. Adds a delay between emails to respect provider limits
 
7. Updates Google Sheets to mark emails as sent

## Key features

- Google Sheets–based contact management

- SMTP email sending (works with any professional provider)

- No-show and status-based filtering

- Built-in duplicate email prevention

- Configurable delay between emails

- Beginner-friendly and easy to customize

- No external email marketing tools required


## Ideal for

- Marketers sending follow-ups or reminders

- Sales teams running targeted outreach

- Coaches and course creators following up with attendees

- Founders and small teams managing email outreach via spreadsheets

- Anyone who wants simple, rule-based email automation

parameters.html:
={{ 'Hey ' + $json.name + ',

' + 'You didn’t attend the last workshop....everything okay at your home?

' + 'I genuinely hope all is well. I was looking forward to helping you move closer to your career goals, so I wanted to check in personally.

' + 'I’m planning another live session, and I’d love to have you join this time so you don’t miss the important steps.

' + '👉 Would you like me to reserve a seat for you in the next session?
' + 'Just reply Yes and I’ll share the details.

' + 'And if anything held you back earlier, feel free to share - I’m here to support you.

' + 'Let’s go win together 💪

' + 'Warm regards,
' + 'Lakshit Bhayana

' + 'Connect with me:
' + 'Instagram | ' + 'YouTube | ' + 'LinkedIn | ' }}
parameters.subject: ={{ 'Hi ' + $json.name + ', quick follow-up after the workshop' }}

nodes.name: Mark email as sent in sheet

parameters.content:
## What to test before running the campaign

✅ Send 2–3 test emails to your own email address
✅ Verify personalization works (Name is replaced correctly)
✅ Check that emails do not land in the spam folder
✅ Confirm the Google Sheet updates Email Sent = Yes after sending
✅ Run the workflow again to ensure already-sent contacts are skipped
✅ Verify email formatting (line breaks, links, signatures)
✅ Test with an empty Name field (use a generic greeting like “Hi there”)

## Email outreach setup

## How it works
- Reads contacts from Google Sheets
- Filters contacts based on no-show or custom status
- Skips contacts already emailed
- Sends personalized emails via SMTP
- Updates the sheet after sending

## Setup steps
- Connect Google Sheets and SMTP credentials
- Prepare a sheet with Email, Name, Status, Email Sent columns
- Test the workflow
- Activate the schedule trigger

## 🚫 SMTP provider requirements (important)

Some consumer email providers restrict or block SMTP usage for automated workflows.

## ✅ Recommended SMTP providers

These services are designed for automated and transactional emails:
	•	SendGrid (free tier available)
	•	Webmail
	•	Custom domain SMTP (cPanel / Plesk hosting)

## ⚠️ Not recommended for automation

These providers may fail or block automated SMTP usage:
	•	Gmail SMTP
	•	Yahoo SMTP
	•	Outlook / Hotmail SMTP

Fetches contacts from Google Sheets.
Ensure column names match
the workflow configuration.

Filters contacts based on
no-show or custom status
and skips already emailed leads.

Prepares personalized email
content and sends emails
using an SMTP provider.

Adds a delay between emails
and updates Google Sheets
to prevent duplicate sends.

## Common first-time issues

❌ No emails sent → Check SMTP credentials and From Email address
❌ Sheet not updating → Confirm the Mark email as sent in sheet node points to the correct spreadsheet and tab
❌ Workflow keeps looping → Ensure Process contacts one by one batch size is set to 1

data.details:
The IF node can be used to implement binary conditional logic in your workflow. You can set up one-to-many conditions to evaluate each item of data being inputted into the node. That data will either evaluate to TRUE or FALSE and route out of the node accordingly.

This node has multiple types of conditions: Bool, String, Number, and Date & Time.
